subject: EU Law
module: Member State Liability and National Procedural Autonomy

lessons.title: The Francovich Principle
lessons.description: Let us begin with the background and the problem that gave rise to the doctrine. Before 1991, the position of an individual harmed by a state's failure to implement a directive was unenviable.

lessons.title: Brasserie, Factortame and the Sufficiently Serious Breach
lessons.description: These cases extended and refined the Francovich principle in ways that are of fundamental importance for FE-1 candidates.

lessons.title: National Procedural Autonomy and Its Limits
lessons.description: This is a topic of fundamental practical importance: when an individual wishes to enforce an EU law right before a national court, the procedural rules governing that claim — time limits, rules of standing, limitations on remedies...
